issues_found: event_type 'fomc_statement' is technically valid per taxonomy but imprecise — this is an Implementation Note, which is operationally distinct from the FOMC policy statement itself (which was issued separately on June 16, 2021)
Source is a historical document from June 2021 (~4 years 9 months old) but has clear archival value as a primary source documenting pandemic-era Fed operational framework

corrected_fields.summary: The Federal Reserve Board unanimously set the interest rate paid on required and excess reserve balances at 0.15 percent, effective June 17, 2021, to support smooth functioning of short-term funding markets. The FOMC directive maintained the federal funds rate target range at 0 to 1/4 percent while continuing asset purchases at $80 billion per month in Treasuries and $40 billion per month in agency MBS.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This implementation note documents the operational details of the Fed's highly accommodative monetary policy stance during mid-2021. The setting of IOER 15 bps above the bottom of the target range was specifically designed to anchor fed funds trading within the target range. The document also confirms ongoing QE at $120 billion per month combined pace and the RRP facility parameters that were critical for managing short-term dollar funding markets.
corrected_fields.macro_context: This document reflects Fed policy from June 2021, when the Federal Reserve was maintaining maximum accommodation following the pandemic emergency rate cuts to zero in March 2020. The operational framework described was designed to keep short-term funding markets functioning properly while pursuing asset purchases to provide additional accommodation. This is a historical record from nearly 5 years prior to the current date and does not reflect current Fed policy.
corrected_fields.market_structure_context: The note describes the technical implementation of monetary policy, including the IOER rate as a floor tool, the RRP facility parameters, and the operational details for MBS settlement including dollar roll and coupon swap transactions. These are market plumbing details related to how the Fed executes its policy stance.
